title: Ceratocephalus caespitosus
intro: Ceratocephalus caespitosus, commonly known as woolly bittercress, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits. Bioactive constituents of Ceratocephalus caespitosus include glucosinolates, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional herbal medicine, woolly bittercress is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
taxonomy: Ceratocephalus caespitosus is classified in the plant kingdom. It belongs to the family Ranunculaceae, commonly known as the buttercup family. This family is part of the order Ranunculales. Within the Ranunculales order, Ceratocephalus caespitosus is further classified as a genus within the tribe Ranunculeae. It is a species native to alpine and subalpine habitats in Europe.
uses: Ceratocephalus caespitosus is a low-growing, alpine plant that has been traditionally used in some cultures for medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Traditional uses also include treating digestive issues and respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds. More studies are required to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and to standardize its use in modern medicine.
